question.text: A trader shorts 8 lots of GBPINR futures at 104.25. The settlement is at 103.50. What is the profit?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Short position: P&L = (Entry - Settlement) × 1000 × Lots = (104.25 - 103.50) × 1000 × 8 = 0.75 × 8000 = Rs. 6,000 profit. **The Trap:** Short profits when price falls. GBPINR lot size is 1000 GBP. **Cross-Exam Context:** Short futures P&L is tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** GBP typically has the highest absolute price among INR pairs, meaning each lot has a higher contract value and higher absolute P&L per point move.
